ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD, leads the UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative targeting digital infrastructure across Africa announced in a G20 context — an Africa-wide financing envelope applicable to Rwanda; no Rwanda-specific deployment is evidenced.

GAP: the CEPA evidence lists in-force partners (India, Indonesia, Israel, Turkey, Cambodia, Georgia, Costa Rica, Mauritius, Serbia, Jordan) and signed-not-in-force partners including Kenya, but contains no record of a UAE–Rwanda CEPA negotiation, signature, or trade-volume figure.

GAP: no evidence in the material documents DP World, AD Ports or Masdar logistics, port, or energy projects in Rwanda; missing is any project record, contract, or announcement tying UAE infrastructure operators to Rwandan facilities.
GAP: Masdar's only evidenced activity is a global offshore wind logistics partnership with AD Ports Group; no Masdar renewable-energy project, PPA, or MOU in Rwanda appears in the material.
GAP: the material contains no UAE–Rwanda diplomatic framework, embassy activity, ministerial statement, or bilateral agreement; missing is any MoFA record naming Rwanda.
GAP: no evidence documents Mubadala, ADQ, or other UAE sovereign or private bilateral investment into Rwanda; missing is any deal value, sector, or counterparty record.
GAP: no evidence in the material addresses a UAE–Rwanda remittance corridor; missing are flow volumes, worker population figures, or payment-channel records.

The UAE is the largest aid provider to Gaza (~50% of all international aid entering Gaza), delivering more than $3 billion since 2023 and over 90,000 tons via 8,700 trucks, 80 airdrops and 13 ships, with UAE-supported facilities treating 75,000 Gazans; operations continued through July 2026 under Operation Chivalrous Knight 3, Gallant Knight 3 and Birds of Goodness, passing the 300-convoy milestone.

The UAE announced a $30 million Sudan emergency response and pledged $500 million to a Sudan Humanitarian Fund in February 2026, and publicly supports WFP operations in Sudan, condemning the targeting of a WFP convoy.
The UAE sent 30 tonnes of food aid to Kenya in February 2026 as direct bilateral food assistance amid deepening La Niña-driven drought and food insecurity.
The UAE and Kenya co-chair the joint ministerial mechanism on Sudan de-escalation alongside the EU and Qatar, and a UAE–Kenya CEPA has been signed but is not yet in force.
The UAE and Qatar both co-signed the Gaza maritime corridor ministerial joint statement and both co-chaired the 24 September 2025 ministerial meeting on Sudan de-escalation.
A 2024 academic study in The International Spectator comparatively analyses Qatar and the UAE as rival Gulf donors in Sudan.

The UAE and the European Commission maintain a working humanitarian-logistics dialogue: DG ECHO co-hosted a Brussels supply-chain resilience panel on 11 June 2026 with 80+ senior representatives including Dubai Humanitarian, the Commission co-signed the Gaza maritime corridor statement with the UAE, and the EU co-chairs the Sudan de-escalation mechanism with the UAE.

The UAE contributed $1.5 million to an ADB fund promoting agricultural innovation and Asia-Pacific food security in May 2026, channeled via the International Affairs Office at the UAE Presidential Court.
UN OCHA independently tracks the UAE (donor #100) in its Financial Tracking Service, recording $94,336,382 in funded and $133,015,246 in paid UAE humanitarian flows for 2026.
AD Ports Group and Masdar formed a November 2025 partnership for global offshore wind logistics.

The UAE–India CEPA (signed February 2022) drove bilateral trade to USD 100 billion in FY2024-25 with a USD 200 billion target by 2032, serving as the template for CEPA impact benchmarking; UAE non-oil foreign trade reached Dh3 trillion in 2024 with CEPAs contributing Dh135bn (+42% YoY).

Sheikh Theyab bin Mohamed bin Zayed — appointed chairman of the International Humanitarian and Philanthropic Council under Federal Decree No. 94 of 2026 — attended the International Development Dialogue hosted by the Office of Development Affairs, which also hosted philanthropy sessions at Investopia 2025.

The UAE President established the UAE Aid Agency by Federal Decree No. 27 of 2024, institutionalizing federal foreign aid alongside the UAE Aid open-data transparency programme, which published the UAE Foreign Aid Report 2024; the UAE has provided more than $100 billion in foreign aid since 1971.
The UAE and the EU co-chaired the September 2025 Sudan de-escalation ministerial and operate a joint co-chair mechanism together with Qatar and Kenya.

The UAE and the European Commission maintain a working humanitarian-logistics dialogue, including a June 2026 Brussels panel on global humanitarian supply-chain resilience co-hosted by DG ECHO with 80+ senior representatives.
Dubai Humanitarian participated in the June 2026 UAE–EU supply-chain resilience discussions in Brussels co-hosted by DG ECHO.
A 2024 academic study in The International Spectator comparatively analyses Qatar and the UAE as rival Gulf donors in Sudan.
The UAE joined the UK, US, Cyprus, Qatar and the European Commission in a joint ministerial statement advancing a maritime corridor to deliver humanitarian assistance to Gaza.
The UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs publicly condemned the targeting of a WFP humanitarian convoy in Sudan and expressed support for WFP operations there.
ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD, leads the UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative targeting digital infrastructure across Africa, announced in a G20 context.
The UAE foreign-aid agency co-leads the $1 billion AI for Development initiative with ADEX, ADFD's export-financing arm, linking UAE development finance to African digital infrastructure.
The UAE contributed $1.5 million in May 2026, via the International Affairs Office at the UAE Presidential Court, to an ADB fund promoting agricultural innovation and Asia-Pacific food security.
The Tony Elumelu Foundation partnered with the UAE Office of Development Affairs and the Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation to empower an additional 1,000 young African entrepreneurs.
The Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation co-funds African entrepreneurship programming with the Tony Elumelu Foundation.

The UAE President issued Federal Decree No. 94 of 2026 restructuring the International Humanitarian and Philanthropic Council, a federal humanitarian coordination body, and appointing Sheikh Theyab bin Mohamed bin Zayed as chairman.
UN OCHA independently tracks UAE humanitarian funding through its Financial Tracking Service, recording $94.3 million in funded and $133 million in paid UAE humanitarian flows for 2026.
The Office of Development Affairs at the UAE Presidential Court held an event with the UAE Embassy in Beijing in July 2026.
The Office of Development Affairs hosted two sessions at Investopia 2025 on the future of philanthropy.
UAE Aid, the federal foreign-aid agency, implements UAE foreign-aid transparency through an open-data portal at uaeaid.ae and publishes official figures including the UAE Foreign Aid Report 2024.
The EU allocated €124 million of its €458 million 2026 Middle East humanitarian package to Palestine, acting as a major donor alongside Gulf donors including the UAE.
